requested_suite_presence.resolution: all requested successor and visibility suites remain present, and this cycle added a direct regression guard for the repaired AutoFix enrichment path

bootstrap_attempts.failure_cause: overlay auth strategy token required FIXOPS_API_TOKEN to be set during clean restart

bootstrap_attempts.resolution: local API restarted successfully with FIXOPS_MODE=enterprise, FIXOPS_API_TOKEN, FIXOPS_JWT_SECRET, and telemetry disabled

notes: the fresh autonomous-cycle baseline reproduced the insecure-deserialization AutoFix HTTP 500 before any code edits were applied

changes_applied.change: added ThreatEnricher interface compatibility so AutoFix graph enrichment works with either enrich(...) or enrich_findings(...)
added a regression test covering the enrich_findings-only ThreatEnricher contract
rewrote the branch status narrative for the repaired AutoFix path and refreshed evidence set
wrote a new machine-readable report capturing the fix, validation evidence, restart contract, and next actions

environment_remediation.rationale: the first post-fix rerun still hit the old error because the patched code was not loaded into the running API process, so the lowest-risk runtime remediation was a clean restart under the repository's required tokenized overlay contract

environment_notes.restart_without_token_failure_cause: overlay auth strategy token required FIXOPS_API_TOKEN

remaining_risks: the high-visibility validation slice passes behaviorally but does not satisfy the repository-wide 18% coverage gate when run alone
the broader foundational validation slice also remains below the repository-wide coverage gate when run in isolation
live backlog findings remain around token expiration, sensitive logging, weak cryptography, insecure deserialization, brain-pipeline robustness, and Dockerfile hygiene
future autonomous cycles will still need the tokenized overlay-compatible restart contract when the local API must be relaunched
additional service call sites may still exist elsewhere if they assume deprecated helper interfaces instead of current contracts

scan for other compatibility assumptions where code still expects deprecated helper interfaces rather than current service contracts
